title: silene inflata subsp. alpina
intro: Silene inflata subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Catchfly, is a plant species in the carnation family that is native to the high-altitude regions of the Pyrenees mountains. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Silene inflata subsp. alpina include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Silene inflata subsp. alpina include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, infections, and digestive issues.
